observe: Nothing in this world waits its turn. When something needs you, it interrupts — no warning.
fault: Feel that? Something just raised its hand mid-step. Ignore it and you lose what it was trying to tell you.
repair: Watch the vector table. Every interrupt line points to a handler — follow it.
escape: It won't wait for you to catch up. Read the trace fast.

observe: Interrupts allow devices to signal the CPU asynchronously.
fault: An interrupt has been raised and must be handled before execution resumes.
repair: Trace the interrupt through the vector table to its service routine.
escape: Process the interrupt trace before it scrolls past.

hints: Each log line follows a strict order — vector lookup happens before the handler runs.
